name: Waldine's Turkey Sausage Gumbo
recipeIngredient: Turkey carcass and bones (leave some meat on when carving)
1 lb smoked or Andouille sausage; 1-inch slices
Leftover gravy, if you have it
1 dash poultry seasoning, thyme, and basil
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, cook flour in the oil over medium heat, stirring until it turns golden brown. Do not burn.
2. Add chopped vegetables (onion, celery, green pepper) and sauté until they become translucent.
3. Incorporate the turkey carcass, bones, leftover turkey pieces, sausage slices, gravy (if using), neck, and giblets. Season with Creole seasoning, poultry seasoning, thyme, and basil, along with salt and pepper to taste.
4. Cook the mixture until all the meat falls off the bones easily.
5. Let the gumbo cool, then remove any remaining bones by hand or using a strainer.
6. Refrigerate the soup to allow excess fat to rise to the surface; skim it off.
7. Stir in fresh parsley (a Thanksgiving leftover) for added flavor.
8. Before serving, thicken the gumbo with instant flour or a mixture of cornstarch and water if desired.
9. Serve over rice.
